title: CNC Hot Wire Foam Cutter
description: Two-axis (X-Y) GRBL-based CNC foam cutter using a tensioned nichrome hot wire on a CoreXY gantry. Cuts EPS and EPP foam sheets up to 500 mm × 400 mm × 100 mm thickness at 3–5 mm/s. Wire temperature controlled via MOSFET PWM on a 12 V supply.
objective: Cut 2D profiles from EPS foam with ≤ 1 mm positional accuracy; 0–100 % wire power control; G-code input via USB serial (GRBL); cutting area ≥ 400 mm × 300 mm.
constraints: 12 V / 5 A power supply; desktop footprint ≤ 600 mm × 500 mm; GRBL-compatible G-code; ≤ £60 BOM.
supporting_fact_titles: Stepper Motor Drive — Full Step and Microstepping
Nichrome Resistance Heating Wire Power Calculation
N-Channel MOSFET as a Low-Side Power Switch
design_element_titles: Nichrome Hot Wire Foam Cutter Drive

element_usage_notes.CoreXY Belt Drive Gantry Architecture: GT2 belt, 16-tooth pulleys; 80 mm/step @ 1/16 microstepping; 500 mm × 400 mm travel.
element_usage_notes.NEMA17 Stepper DRV8825 Microstepping Dri: 1/16 microstepping; 1.5 A current limit; X and Y axes; 3D-printed carriage mounts.
element_usage_notes.Nichrome Hot Wire Foam Cutter Drive: 200 mm span of 28 AWG NiCr wire; 12 V supply via IRLZ44N; GRBL spindle PWM (pin 11) maps 0–100 % to wire power.
element_usage_notes.GRBL G-code CNC Firmware Controller: GRBL 1.1h flashed to Arduino Uno; spindle-enable maps to wire MOSFET gate; $130=500 and $131=400 (work area mm).
element_usage_notes.Arduino Uno/Nano MCU Platform: Arduino Uno R3; GRBL firmware; USB-B connector to PC; CNC Shield v3 for DRV8825 drivers.

element_interactions.data: STEP/DIR pulses at up to 30 kHz per axis
element_interactions.description: GRBL generates step/direction pulses for X and Y axes. DRV8825 drivers on CNC Shield receive STEP and DIR; MS0/MS1/MS2 set to 1/16 microstepping.
GRBL spindle-speed output (0–255 PWM) drives IRLZ44N gate via 100 Ω series resistor. M3 Sxxx sets wire power %; M5 turns wire off.

element_interactions.to: Nichrome Hot Wire Foam Cutter Drive
element_interactions.data: Switched 12 V current (0–3 A) to nichrome wire
element_interactions.description: MOSFET drain switches the 12 V return of the nichrome wire. PWM duty cycle modulates mean power and thus wire temperature.

element_interactions.to: Nichrome Hot Wire Foam Cutter Drive
element_interactions.description: Gantry carries the tensioned nichrome wire across the foam blank. CoreXY kinematics allow diagonal cuts without speed variations that would cause uneven cuts.

integration_code:
# cnc_foam_cutter.py — PC-side G-code sender for the CNC Hot Wire Foam Cutter
# Connects to GRBL on the Arduino Uno over USB serial.
# Usage: python cnc_foam_cutter.py --port COM3 --file shape.nc --wire-power 70

import argparse
import time
import serial


def grbl_send(ser: serial.Serial, line: str) -> str:
    """Send one G-code line to GRBL and wait for 'ok' or 'error'."""
    cmd = (line.strip() + '\n').encode()
    ser.write(cmd)
    while True:
        resp = ser.readline().decode().strip()
        if resp in ('ok', '') or resp.startswith('error'):
            return resp


def main() -> None:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description='CNC Hot Wire Foam Cutter sender')
    parser.add_argument('--port',        required=True, help='Serial port (e.g. COM3 or /dev/ttyUSB0)')
    parser.add_argument('--baud',        type=int, default=115200)
    parser.add_argument('--file',        required=True, help='G-code file path')
    parser.add_argument('--wire-power',  type=int, default=70, help='Wire power 0-100 %%')
    parser.add_argument('--feed-rate',   type=int, default=200, help='Feed rate mm/min')
    args = parser.parse_args()

    with serial.Serial(args.port, args.baud, timeout=5) as ser:
        time.sleep(2)  # wait for GRBL to initialise
        ser.flushInput()

        # GRBL soft reset + unlock
        ser.write(b'\x18')  # Ctrl-X reset
        time.sleep(1)
        grbl_send(ser, '$X')   # kill alarm lock
        grbl_send(ser, 'G21')  # metric mode
        grbl_send(ser, 'G90')  # absolute positioning
        grbl_send(ser, f'F{args.feed_rate}')  # default feed rate

        # Enable hot wire at requested power
        spindle_s = int(args.wire_power * 255 / 100)
        grbl_send(ser, f'M3 S{spindle_s}')   # spindle on = wire on
        print(f'Wire power set to {args.wire_power} % (S={spindle_s})')
        time.sleep(3)  # allow wire to reach temperature

        # Stream G-code file
        with open(args.file) as f:
            lines = [ln.strip() for ln in f if ln.strip() and not ln.startswith(';')]

        for i, line in enumerate(lines, 1):
            resp = grbl_send(ser, line)
            if resp.startswith('error'):
                print(f'GRBL error on line {i}: {line!r} → {resp}')
                break
            if i % 20 == 0:
                print(f'  {i}/{len(lines)} lines sent...')

        # Wire off and return to home
        grbl_send(ser, 'M5')       # wire off
        grbl_send(ser, 'G0 X0 Y0') # return home
        print('Cut complete. Wire off, at home position.')


if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()
